name: Kim Chi
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the outer leaves from the cabbage and place them together with the rest of the cabbage in a large bowl. Sprinkle salt over everything. Let stand for 3 hours, stirring occasionally.
2. Peel the daikon and cut into long thin strips. Cut onions into 1" strips, then shred lengthwise into slivers. Mix daikon and onion strips separately and let sit while cabbage and salt mixture is setting.
3. Peel and mince garlic and gingerroot. Cut celery into 1-inch lengths and shred lengthwise. Peel, core, and slice the pear, then cut into long strips. Combine these ingredients with cayenne pepper and granulated sugar. Mix into daikon and onion mixture.
4. The cabbage will produce a brine after setting. Remove outer leaves from brine and set aside. Take one quarter of the cabbage head, rinse under running water, and pack daikon and onion mixture between leaves. Place in bottom of crock or container. Repeat with remaining quarters. Layer any leftover mixture over cabbage.
5. Press down on kimchi. Cover tightly and let sit for 3 days. The longer it ferments, the stronger the flavor becomes. Store in a cool place (60°F) until ready to use. Once opened, store in glass jars. For spicier kim chi, add diced red peppers with seeds to daikon and onion mixture before packing; proceed at your own risk.
